prompt: I grow apples. Create a simple listing for my Honeycrisp apples with the price, weight, and where they were grown.
Now I want to list all my produce — apples, tomatoes, and herbs — with prices and quantities for each.
Group my produce into categories: fruits, vegetables, and herbs. Each category should be browsable.
Build my complete farm stand module. Include my produce collections, my farm name and logo, and a way for customers to contact me.
I'm at the Saturday market. Connect my stand to the other vendors nearby.
A clothing vendor and a jewelry maker just arrived at the market.
I'm packing up. Remove my stand from the market.
Show me my full apple inventory with cost per unit, supplier info, profit margins, and stock counts.
My supplier wants to see what I have available for wholesale.
A customer at the market is browsing my stand from their phone.
I'm at the park and want to see what people around me are reading.
I just walked into the library. Connect my reading list automatically.
Build a module that watches nearby public nodes, shows what services they expose, and lets me browse them by type.
When I arrive at a pop-up market, assemble a live view of the stands around me and remove them when they leave.
The city parks department wants to show local art on digital screens in the park. Artists connect their portfolios when they're nearby.
I'm at the farmer's market. Show me what's available, but overlay my dietary restrictions. I'm diabetic.
As a city planner, show me a real-time heatmap of fresh food availability across all active farmer's markets in the metro area.
